criteria.criterion: A completion close whose HEAD moves to an UNRELATED commit between verify and sign does NOT produce a closed-without-signature ticket — either it pins the verified rev and signs it, or it aborts the close and leaves the ticket in_progress (retryable). Verified by a test that moves HEAD in the verify→sign window.
There is a non-`--force`, non-fabrication recovery to obtain the completion attestation for a closed-but-unsigned ticket (or the close never lands unsigned on a benign race in the first place).
The residual unsigned-close message (if any) names an ACTUALLY-WORKING recovery, not a no-op re-close.

citation.description: Both test files (test_close_default_pins_head_4de6.py, test_close_drift_message_4de6.py) are complete, non-stubbed, and contain no skip/xfail markers. The implementation in transition_close.py is complete. Cannot execute make targets directly, but no evidence of breakage.

criteria.criterion: make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
